name: Walt Pearson
description: An old man on a porch step turning a ring of picks over in his hands, watching the cul-de-sac with the patience of someone who has nowhere left to be.
background: Neighbourhood locksmith for forty years; there is not a door on Maple Street he cannot open.
personality: Dry, watchful, and slow to warm — he measures a person before he'll hand over a key.
secret: He can't sleep in his own house anymore, so he lets himself into the neighbours' empty ones — and takes one small keepsake from each, a grief he calls tidying up.

knowledge.hint: There's a gun shop in the strip mall off Overlook — steel door, drop bar, the works. I cut keys for the owner for fifteen years. That lock and I are old friends.
The Garden Center up on Overlook still had its loading dock stacked with seed and dry goods when the roads closed. Nobody thinks to raid a garden center.

notes: T45 · The Terraces. Skill: locks/entry. Hook: access to sealed homes/caches (FR-NPC-06); the quiet theft is grief, not menace. T53: both leads reveal real, NON-adjacent lootable terraces nodes (the strip-mall gun shop, the Garden Center) — reachable because you ask Walt at maple-street, out of sight of both.
